description: A 60-item assessment producing a 16-type personality classification based on Jungian psychological types, measuring Extraversion/Introversion, Sensing/Intuition, Thinking/Feeling, and Judging/Perceiving dimensions.

consentBlock.title: Open Extended Jungian Type Scales (OEJTS)
consentBlock.bodyText: This assessment contains 60 statements about your preferences and tendencies. For each statement, indicate how much you agree or disagree. There are no right or wrong answers. Your responses will be used to determine your Jungian personality type as part of your Legacy Portrait. Your data is stored securely and encrypted. This instrument is based on open-source items from Open Source Psychometrics Project.
consentBlock.requiredCheckboxLabel: I understand this is a self-report personality type assessment and consent to my responses being stored and scored.

disclaimerText: This assessment is for personal insight and legacy-building purposes only. It is not a clinical diagnostic tool. Based on open-source items from the Open Source Psychometrics Project (openpsychometrics.org).

EI.text: You show a clear preference for Extraversion, drawing energy from social interaction and external activity.
You show a balanced preference between Extraversion and Introversion.
You show a clear preference for Introversion, drawing energy from solitude and internal reflection.

SN.text: You show a clear preference for Sensing, focusing on concrete facts and present realities.
You show a balanced preference between Sensing and Intuition.
You show a clear preference for Intuition, focusing on patterns, possibilities, and future potential.

TF.text: You show a clear preference for Thinking, making decisions based on logic and objective analysis.
You show a balanced preference between Thinking and Feeling.
You show a clear preference for Feeling, making decisions based on personal values and how others will be affected.

JP.text: You show a clear preference for Judging, preferring structure, planning, and decisiveness.
You show a balanced preference between Judging and Perceiving.
You show a clear preference for Perceiving, preferring flexibility, spontaneity, and keeping options open.

videoPromptTrigger: Reflect on the preferences you just indicated. Record a brief video sharing how these tendencies show up in your relationships and daily decisions.

domainDescriptions.EI: The Extraversion-Introversion dimension describes where you direct your energy. Extraverts are energized by the outer world of people and activity, while Introverts draw energy from their inner world of ideas and reflection. This dimension, central to Jung's theory of psychological types, influences communication style, social preferences, and how you process experiences.
domainDescriptions.SN: The Sensing-Intuition dimension describes how you take in information. Sensors focus on concrete facts, details, and present realities, while Intuitives focus on patterns, possibilities, and future potential. This dimension shapes learning style, problem-solving approach, and what kind of information you naturally notice and trust.
domainDescriptions.TF: The Thinking-Feeling dimension describes how you make decisions. Thinkers prioritize logic, consistency, and objective analysis, while Feelers prioritize values, harmony, and the impact on people. This dimension influences conflict resolution style, leadership approach, and how you evaluate choices.
domainDescriptions.JP: The Judging-Perceiving dimension describes how you orient to the outer world. Judgers prefer structure, planning, and closure, while Perceivers prefer flexibility, spontaneity, and keeping options open. This dimension influences work style, time management, and how comfortable you are with ambiguity.
domainDescriptions.extraversion_pole: Extraversion in Jungian typology reflects orientation toward the external world. High scorers are energized by social interaction, think out loud, and prefer breadth of experience. They tend to act first and reflect later, and are often described as outgoing and expressive.
domainDescriptions.introversion_pole: Introversion reflects orientation toward the inner world of thoughts and ideas. High scorers prefer depth over breadth, think before speaking, and need solitude to recharge. They tend to be reflective, reserved, and focused on internal experiences.
domainDescriptions.sensing_pole: Sensing reflects a preference for concrete, tangible information gathered through the five senses. High scorers are practical, detail-oriented, and grounded in present reality. They trust direct experience and prefer step-by-step instructions over abstract theories.
domainDescriptions.intuition_pole: Intuition reflects a preference for abstract patterns, meanings, and future possibilities. High scorers are imaginative, conceptual thinkers who see the big picture and connections between ideas. They trust hunches and are drawn to innovation and theoretical frameworks.
domainDescriptions.thinking_pole: Thinking reflects a preference for making decisions based on logical analysis and objective criteria. High scorers value fairness, consistency, and truth. They tend to be direct in communication and may prioritize task completion over interpersonal harmony.
domainDescriptions.feeling_pole: Feeling reflects a preference for making decisions based on personal values and the impact on others. High scorers value harmony, empathy, and compassion. They are attuned to others' emotions and prioritize maintaining positive relationships.
domainDescriptions.judging_pole: Judging reflects a preference for structure, organization, and decisiveness. High scorers like to plan ahead, meet deadlines, and bring things to closure. They feel most comfortable when decisions are made and plans are in place.
domainDescriptions.perceiving_pole: Perceiving reflects a preference for flexibility, adaptability, and openness to new information. High scorers prefer to keep their options open, adapt to changing circumstances, and may find rigid schedules constraining. They are often energized by last-minute challenges.
